schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: HYBRID INFORMATION CAPACITY Canonical Statutory Formula

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 1168] HYBRID INFORMATION CAPACITY Canonical Statutory Formula: $C_{TZP} \approx S_{temp} + S_{atemp} + I_{ent} \approx 17 \text{ bits}$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Establishes the specific information density of a TZP transition. It combines classical measurement capacity (~5-7 bits), quantum capacity (~5-7 qubits), and entanglement entropy (~3-5 bits) to yield the distinct signature of ~17 bits per state transition, exceeding standard Shannon or Holevo limits. Source: [10], [11], [12]
theory.technical_interpretation: HYBRID INFORMATION CAPACITY Canonical Statutory Formula is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written C_TZP ≈ S_temp + S_atemp + I_ent ≈ 17 bits. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from C_TZP, S_temp, S_atemp, I_ent. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the source where [ID 1168] HYBRID INFORMATION CAPACITY Canonical Statutory Formula
